summary: Increased risk of bleeding, including GI hemorrhage.
plainLanguage: Taking these together can make you bleed more easily, even from small cuts. Avoid this combination.
Both thin your blood. Together they raise the risk of dangerous bleeding.
Naproxen and warfarin together can cause serious bleeding.

summary: ACE inhibitors raise potassium; supplements can cause hyperkalemia.
plainLanguage: Adding potassium supplements with this blood pressure pill can raise potassium too high.

summary: NSAIDs may reduce lisinopril effect and impair kidney function.
plainLanguage: Ibuprofen can make your blood pressure pill work less well and stress your kidneys.
Drinking alcohol with metformin can cause a rare but serious buildup of acid in the blood.

summary: Grapefruit juice raises simvastatin levels and muscle damage risk.
plainLanguage: Avoid grapefruit juice — it makes this cholesterol pill stronger and can hurt your muscles.
Together these can cause a dangerous reaction called serotonin syndrome.

summary: SSRIs with NSAIDs increase GI bleeding risk.
plainLanguage: These together can cause stomach bleeding more easily.
Drinking alcohol with Tylenol can hurt your liver.
This antibiotic can make methotrexate too strong.
Omeprazole can make this blood thinner work less well.
Together these can cause dangerous heart rhythm problems.

summary: Calcium reduces levothyroxine absorption — separate doses by 4 hours.
plainLanguage: Take your thyroid pill at least 4 hours apart from calcium.
Iron pills can block your thyroid medicine. Space them out.
These together can cause a dangerous serotonin reaction.
Fluoxetine can make warfarin's blood-thinning effect stronger.

summary: Ibuprofen may reduce cardioprotective effect of low-dose aspirin.
plainLanguage: Ibuprofen can block aspirin's heart-protecting effect. Take aspirin first.

summary: Combined risk of bradycardia and heart block.
plainLanguage: Together these can dangerously slow your heart rate.
This combination can cause a life-threatening drop in blood pressure.
